headline: French presidential election: Who's already running, who could be, and key dates
description: Our comprehensive roundup of who has declared they are running, and what potential candidates have said, one year ahead of the vote.

40 years after Chernobyl: In Kazakhstan, the forgotten memory of the 'liquidators'
description: In the days following the explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant on April 26, 1986, more than 30,000 Kazakhs were sent to the Ukrainian site to help with the cleanup. Those who survived the radiation are finding it increasingly difficult to be acknowledged as victims, even as their country has embarked on a civilian nuclear program.

headline: Syrian court conducts first hearing in trial of Bashar al-Assad
description: The ousted ruler and his brother have fled Syria and will be tried in absentia. 'Today we begin the first trials of transitional justice in Syria,' announced the judge as he opened the session.

headline: Bruce, the disabled parrot who became king
description: Deprived of the upper half of his beak, this New Zealand kea, rescued by the Willowbank Wildlife Reserve in 2013, has developed a unique and unstoppable fighting technique.

headline: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
description: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?

description: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.

headline: Annette Messager: 'Without art, I would have become a very mean woman'
description: 'I wouldn't be where I am today if...' In this series, Le Monde interviews a public figure about a decisive moment in their life.  The internationally renowned visual artist recalls the strange atmosphere of the seaside town of Berck, where she grew up.

headline: Washington's resentment toward 'paper tiger' NATO is growing
description: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity with the United States during the war with Iran. Its frustration is particularly strong toward Spain, France and the United Kingdom.
